title: Cozy Buttery Tunisian Lamb Tagine
description: A hearty and aromatic dish featuring ground cumin and tender lamb, cooked to perfection on a cedar plank, this recipe is sugar free friendly and perfect for a cozy night in.
instructions: Preheat the oven to 300 degrees Fahrenheit.
Season the ground lamb with salt, black pepper, and ground cumin.
Heat 2 tablespoons of butter in a large skillet over medium heat and cook the lamb until browned, breaking it up into small pieces as it cooks.
Add the chopped onion and minced garlic to the skillet and cook until the onion is translucent.
Stir in the chopped dried apricots and chicken broth, and bring the mixture to a simmer.
Place the cedar plank on a baking sheet and spoon the lamb mixture onto the plank.
Dot the top of the lamb mixture with the remaining 2 tablespoons of butter.
Cover the baking sheet with aluminum foil and steam the tagine in the preheated oven for 1 hour.
Remove the foil and continue to cook the tagine for an additional 30 minutes, or until the meat is tender and the top is lightly browned.
